message:
⚠️ Complete solution→fixer rename: scripts, copilot instructions, cleanup (#1903)

- Delete legacy solutions/ directory (9 files from before the rename)
- Fix generate-cncf-install-missions.mjs: SOLUTIONS_DIR → fixes/cncf-install
- Fix generate-platform-missions.mjs: SOLUTIONS_DIR → fixes/platform-install
- Fix copilot-instructions.md: solutions/ → fixes/, missionClass "solution" → "fixer"
- Fix platform-install-gen.yml: rename misleading step names

This was causing all 29 open CNCF mission PRs to dual-write files
in both solutions/ (old, wrong) and fixes/ (new, correct) directories.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -5,8 +5,8 @@
 This is a knowledge-base repository containing **kc-mission-v1** troubleshooting mission files as JSON.
 
 ```
-solutions/
-  cncf-generated/<project-name>/<slug>.json   # Auto-generated missions
+fixes/
+  cncf-generated/<project-name>/<slug>.json   # Auto-generated fixer missions
   cncf-install/<slug>.json                     # Install missions
   llm-d/<slug>.json                            # LLM-D missions
   platform-install/<slug>.json                 # Platform install missions
@@ -44,7 +44,7 @@ Every file must be valid `kc-mission-v1`:
 {
   "version": "kc-mission-v1",
   "name": "slug-name",
-  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
   "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
   "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
   "mission": {

@@ -94,7 +94,7 @@ jobs:
       - name: Install dependencies
         run: cd scripts && npm ci --silent
 
-      - name: Create solutions directory
+      - name: Create fixes directory
         run: mkdir -p fixes/platform-install
 
       - name: Generate platform missions
@@ -140,7 +140,7 @@ jobs:
           pattern: platform-missions-batch-*
           merge-multiple: true
 
-      - name: Move mission files to solutions dir
+      - name: Move mission files to fixes dir
         run: |
           mkdir -p fixes/platform-install
           # Move any missions that ended up at repo root

@@ -34,7 +34,7 @@ const BATCH_SIZE = parseInt(process.env.BATCH_SIZE || '20', 10)
 const FORCE_REGENERATE = process.env.FORCE_REGENERATE === 'true'
 const QUALITY_THRESHOLD = parseInt(process.env.QUALITY_THRESHOLD || '60', 10)
 const DRAFT_THRESHOLD = parseInt(process.env.DRAFT_THRESHOLD || '40', 10)
-const SOLUTIONS_DIR = join(process.cwd(), 'solutions', 'cncf-install')
+const SOLUTIONS_DIR = join(process.cwd(), 'fixes', 'cncf-install')
 
 const LLM_ENDPOINT = process.env.LLM_ENDPOINT || 'https://models.inference.ai.azure.com/chat/completions'
 const LLM_MODEL = process.env.LLM_MODEL || 'gpt-4o-mini'

```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."

-        "description": "Inspect the relevant cilium configuration:
```bash

kubectl get configmap -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cilium
```
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the existing issues

### Version

higher than v1.16.0 and lower than v1.17.0

### What happened?

 helm install cilium cilium -n kube-system
Error: INSTALLATION FAILED: 1 error"

-      {

---

  enforceHttps: true
  loadbalancerMode: dedicated

-{
-  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
-  "name": "cloud-custodian-842-access-key-groupings",
-  "missionClass": "solution",
-  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
-  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
-  "mission": {
-    "title": "cloud-custodian: Access Key Groupings",
-    "description": "Access Key Groupings. Requested by 5+ users.",
-    "type": "feature",
-    "status": "completed",
-    "steps": [
-      {
-        "title": "Check current cloud-custodian deployment",
-        "description": "Verify your cloud-custodian version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cloud-custodian -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-custodian
helm list -n cloud-custodian 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cloud-custodian installation."
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Review cloud-custodian configuration",
-        "description": "Inspect the relevant cloud-custodian configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cloud-custodian -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-custodian
kubectl get configmap -n cloud-custodian -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cloud-custodian
```
If an IAM account has never been used the "access_keys.last_used_date" will be absent.  I want to disable access keys that have never been used AND the last rotated key is greater than 60 days."
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Apply the fix for Access Key Groupings",
-        "description": "parse is not needed as k['CreateDate'] is already a datetime object.

Adjusting the policy in https://github.com/capitalone/cloud-custodian/issues/842 and setting the `action` phase to the following solves this issue.

```
    actions:
      - type: remove-keys
        disable: true
        age:
```yaml
name: iam_accounts-disable-never_used
    description: |
       Disable IAM accounts that have never been used in the last 60 days
    resource: iam-user
    filters:
      - type: credential
        key: access_keys.active
        value: true
      - type: credential
        key: access_keys.last_used_date
        value: absent
      - type: credential
        key: access_keys.last_rotated
        value_type: age
        value: 60
        op: gte
    actions:
      - type: remove-keys
        age: 60
        disable: true
```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Verify the feature works",
-        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cloud-custodian -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-custodian
kubectl get events -n cloud-custodian --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Access Key Groupings" is functioning correctly."
-      }
-    ],
-    "resolution": {
-      "summary": "parse is not needed as k['CreateDate'] is already a datetime object.

Adjusting the policy in https://github.com/capitalone/cloud-custodian/issues/842 and setting the `action` phase to the following solves this issue.

```
    actions:
      - type: remove-keys
        disable: true
        age: 90
```",
-      "codeSnippets": [
-        "name: iam_accounts-disable-never_used
    description: |
       Disable IAM accounts that have never been used in the last 60 days
    resource: iam-user
    filters:
      - type: credential
        key: access_keys.active
        value: true
      - type: credential
        key: access_keys.last_used_date
        value: absent
      - type: credential
        key: access_keys.last_rotated
        value_type: age
        value: 60
        op: gte
    actions:
      - type: remove-keys
        age: 60
        disable: true",
-        "[
  {
    "UserName": "read-s3",
    "c7n:credential-report": {
      "password_next_rotation": null,
      "password_enabled": false,
      "access_keys": [
        {
          "active": true,
          "last_rotated": "2016-12-23T15:55:44+00:00",
          "last_used_date": null,
          "last_used_service": null,
          "last_used_region": null
        },
        {
          "active": true,
          "last_rotated": "2016-09-26T18:39:07+00:00",
          "last_used_region": null,
          "last_used_date": "2017-01-09T23:21:00+00:00",
          "last_used_service": "s3"
        }
      ],
      "user_creation_time": "2016-09-26T18:39:07+00:00",
      "mfa_active": false,
      "arn": "arn:aws:iam::xxxxxxx:user/read-s3",
      "password_last_used": null,
      "password_last_change",
-        "actions:
      - type: remove-keys
        disable: true
        age: 90"
-      ]
-    }
-  },

-    "steps": [
-      {

-        "description": "Check for the issue in your keda deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda -n keda --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."

-      {
-        "title": "Apply the fix for Support Sidecar Processes",
-        "description": "I'd like to also better understand how the two containers relate, and why two containers are better than two processes in one container.\n\n1. What happens if one container dies and not the other?\n2. One container will be scaled and driven by requests. If we turned off the CPU between requests, would\n```yaml\napiVersion: v1\nkind: Pod\nmetadata:\n  name: test\nspec:\n  volumes:\n  - name: sock\n    emptyDir: {}\n  containers:\n  - name: nginx\n    image: test\n    imagePullPolicy: Never\n    command: [\"/usr/sbin/nginx\", \"-g daemon off;\"]\n    volumeMounts:\n      - name: sock\n        mountPath: /sock/\n  - name: php-fpm\n    image: test\n    imagePullPolicy: Never\n    command: [\"php-fpm\"]\n    volumeMounts:\n      - name: sock\n        mountPath: /sock/\n```"

-      }
-    ],

-      "summary": "I'd like to also better understand how the two containers relate, and why two containers are better than two processes in one container.\n\n1. What happens if one container dies and not the other?\n2. One container will be scaled and driven by requests. If we turned off the CPU between requests, would that cause a problem?\n3. Do you need any sort of shared disk/etc between the two processes?\n4.",
-      "codeSnippets": [
-        "apiVersion: v1\nkind: Pod\nmetadata:\n  name: test\nspec:\n  volumes:\n  - name: sock\n    emptyDir: {}\n  containers:\n  - name: nginx\n    image: test\n    imagePullPolicy: Never\n    command: [\"/usr/sbin/nginx\", \"-g daemon off;\"]\n    volumeMounts:\n      - name: sock\n        mountPath: /sock/\n  - name: php-fpm\n    image: test\n    imagePullPolicy: Never\n    command: [\"php-fpm\"]\n    volumeMounts:\n      - name: sock\n        mountPath: /sock/"
-      ]
-    }

-    "steps": [

-        "title": "Identify nats analyze symptoms",
-        "description": "Check for the issue in your nats deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats -n nats --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."

-        "title": "Review nats configuration",

-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Apply the fix for Performance degradation with slow consumers [v2.9.X,",
-        "description": "Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:\n```yaml\ndocker build -t test/nats:latest .\n```"

-      {
-        "title": "Confirm Performance degradation with slow consumers is resolved",
-        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats -n nats --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n nats --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
-      }
